calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Dec-09-2021 Continued To Jul-11-22 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-06-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances, not contested, and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Schneider Electric USA, Inc.'s (FKA Square D Company) Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of showing that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that decedent Bruce Hiell was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to propound any "state all facts" interrogatories regarding Defendant's product. (Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2006) 143 Cal. App. 4th 1433, 1440.) Defendant also failed to establish that Gerry Bowden, decedent's co-worker, lacks information supporting Plaintiffs' claims. Although Mr. Bowden's deposition was never taken, Defendant failed to re-notice Mr. Bowden's deposition, request an IDC with the Court, or file a motion to compel, and instead waited 9 days before the deadline to file a motion for summary judgment to seek clarification. The Court did not consider Bartholomew v. Triay (2014) No. A139998, 2014 WL 3531015, cited by Defendant, as this is an unpublished case. (CRC rule 8.1115.) Additionally, Defendant's separate statement fails to include any reference to page and line numbers in its citation to evidence, as required by CRC 3.1350 (d). Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

Plaintiff'S Motion For Order Maintaining The Current Trial Date With Preference (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than July 15, 2020 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than July 15, 2020 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on September 2, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than September 4, 2020 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than September 18, 2020. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by October 9, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, October 15, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION FOR PREFERENCE / PREFERENTIAL SETTING
Off Calendar Jul-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily Anselmo of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing through CourtCall for defendant CBS Corporation and General Electric Co.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Foster Wheeler.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER MAINTAINING THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE WITH PREFERENCE
No opposition and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Preference is GRANTED pursuant to CCP 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date of November 9, 2020 is vacated and the new trial date is October 19, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 29, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is October 15, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is October 2, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is October 16, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 31, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)
